coverage_note: Exact finite tie-aware law is a positive paper claim.
claim_text: Exact finite tie-aware Best-of-N laws predict selected utility on finite object-candidate populations.

coverage_note: Selected-tail binding failure is a positive paper claim.
claim_text: In controlled object-centric scenes, high-N selection can increase object score while real utility stagnates or falls due to binding failures.

coverage_note: Object-specific repair improvements are positive paper claims.
claim_text: Identity, hidden-property, and targeted-probe repairs improve selected utility in the controlled synthetic setting.

coverage_note: CPU NumPy semi-learned object-centric evidence is a positive paper claim.
claim_text: A CPU NumPy semi-learned object-centric model improves property, identity-alignment, and transition prediction over simple baselines on generated trajectories.

coverage_note: Real-robot validation is explicitly not claimed.
claim_text: The method is validated on real robot systems.

coverage_note: Broad benchmark superiority is explicitly not claimed.
claim_text: The method establishes broad benchmark superiority over graph physics, latent, or diffusion world models.
